     The Numbers Business Report

District 9 (2009)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $115,646,235Details
International Box Office $96,807,196
Worldwide Box Office $212,453,431
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $32,748,781
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $24,735,365
Total $57,484,146
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Thirty years ago, aliens arrive on Earth — not to conquer or give aid, but — to find refuge from their dying planet. Separated from humans in a South African area called District 9, the aliens are managed by Multi-National United, which is unconcerned with the aliens’ welfare but will do anything to master their advanced technology. When a company field agent contracts a mysterious virus that begins to alter his DNA, there is only one place he can hide: District 9.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$37,354,308 (32.3% of total gross)
Legs:3.10 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:54.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$30,000,000 (worldwide box office is 7.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,049 opening theaters/3,180 max. theaters, 6.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $184,417,196

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 14th, 2009 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
Video Release: December 22nd, 2009 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for bloody violence and pervasive language
Running Time: 111 minutes
Award nomination: 2010 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Cast, crew, or production detail: Director Remaking Their Own Movie, Motion Capture Performance, Remake
Plot point: First Contact, Friendly Alien on Earth
Subgenre: Action Thriller
Source:Based on Short Film
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Wingnut Films
Production Countries: New Zealand, United States, South Africa
Languages: English, Nyanja

Synopsis

Thirty years ago, aliens arrive on Earth — not to conquer or give aid, but — to find refuge from their dying planet. Separated from humans in a South African area called District 9, the aliens are managed by Multi-National United, which is unconcerned with the aliens’ welfare but will do anything to master their advanced technology. When a company field agent contracts a mysterious virus that begins to alter his DNA, there is only one place he can hide: District 9.
